notes:
PRIMARY SOURCE (re-fetched, confirmed): Flipside Gaming empirical opening of 720 packs / 20 booster boxes of Ultra Prism. Reported per-box: standard GX 2.95, Prism Star 3.15, full-art Ultra Rare 1.55, Secret Rare 0.65. CROSS-CHECK (re-fetched, confirmed): ThePriceDex per-box GX 3, Ultra Rare 1.6, Rainbow 0.4, Secret 0.4 — corroborates within sampling noise.

COARSE-BUCKET MAPPING (verified against pokemontcg.io sm5 API rarity counts, which I fetched directly): Common 45 -> common(45); Uncommon 48 -> uncommon(44, engine bucket); Rare 23 + Rare Holo 13 + Rare Prism Star 6 = 42 -> rare(42, EXACT match to engine bucket — there is no 'prism' bucket so Prism Star folds into rare); Rare Holo GX 7 + Rare Ultra 19 = 26 -> ultra-rare(25, within 1 of engine bucket); Rare Rainbow 9 + Rare Secret 8 = 17 -> secret-rare(17, EXACT). This proves the engine ultra-rare bucket INCLUDES standard GX, per the TCGdex lumping rule (all GX + full-art -> ultra-rare).

CORRECTED rare-or-better distribution: ultra-rare = (standard GX 2.95 + full-art Ultra Rare 1.55)/36 = 4.50/36 = 0.125; secret-rare = (rainbow + gold/secret) 0.65/36 = 0.018056; rare (incl. Prism Star 3.15/box + holo + non-holo rares) = remainder = 0.856944. Sums to exactly 1.000000.

Hit economy sanity: 4.5 ultra-rare-class hits/box (~1 per 8 packs) and 0.65 secret/box — both pulled straight from Flipside, not invented; no GX-every-pack absurdity.

Pack: 10 cards (5 commons, 3 uncommons, 1 reverse-holo modeled as 50/50 common/uncommon filler, 1 rare-or-better) + 1 basic energy (not modeled). Standard SM set, so shipEv=true. Confidence MEDIUM: one sizeable community sample (720 packs) cross-checked against a second source, but no published large-scale study.
